creator: j@uriah.heep.sax.de
text:
As root@ultima.thenerve.com wrote:

> 	Whenever I run a C compile on anything that requires a bunch
> of files built (or a fairly complex file that includes a bunch of
> files, etc..), then cc1 either crashes with an error message, saying
> it got a SIGSEGV if it's being nice. If being its usual nasty self,
> the machine crashes - the only one time I was at the console (it's
> 350 miles away in NYC), I saw something about an invalid page -
> machine rebooted before I could write it down.

This heavily smells like a hardware problem.  The usual questions...
ah well, the xref to the sig11 web page has recently been added to
the FreeBSD FAQ.  Look at the updated FAQ on www.freebsd.org!

-- 
cheers, J"org

joerg_wunsch@uriah.heep.sax.de -- http://www.sax.de/~joerg/ -- NIC: JW11-RIPE
Never trust an operating system you don't have sources for. ;-)
